title: Polymarket reportedly paid creators to post deceptive videos about fake bets
slides: Polymarket paid creators for fake bet videos—who else knew?
The platform allegedly funded misleading content to boost hype.
Those videos included bets that never actually existed.
Now users must question every payout story they see.
Verify any bet claim before trusting the source.
thread_text:
1. Who funded the lie?

Polymarket paid creators for videos promoting fake bets, fueling hype around non-existent wagers.

2. The hidden contract

The platform allegedly reimbursed users for misleading content, turning fiction into marketing material.

3. The phantom bets

Those polished payout stories showcased wagers that never existed, deceiving audiences into believing a false reality.

4. Trust is now broken

Every viral payout story becomes suspect—users can't tell what's real or bought. The platform's credibility collapses.

5. Verify one bet today

Pick a trending payout claim. Cross-check its existence on the platform. Can you confirm it? If not, trust nothing.
video_script: Polymarket paid for fake bet videos.
Now every payout story is suspect.
Verify any claim before you trust.

instagram.caption:
Polymarket paid creators to post videos about fake bets that never existed on its platform. 💸

Those polished payout stories were marketing fiction, not real wagers.
The platform allegedly reimbursed users for misleading content to drive hype.
Now every viral bet claim is suspect—was it real or bought? 🔍

If you create or publish content, verify every claim before repeating it, because fabricated stories can look authentic.

Pick one trending payout story today. Can you confirm it actually happened on Polymarket? If not, what else can you trust? ⚠️
